rationale: BPC-157 supports localized tissue repair signaling while TB-500 supports repair-cell migration and wound-healing pathways.
expectedSynergy: Complementary repair pathways: BPC-157 emphasizes angiogenic and fibroblast/tendon signaling; TB-500 emphasizes repair-cell migration and inflammatory resolution.
safetyCaveats: No direct high-quality human combination trial found. Keep this as research metadata, not a therapeutic protocol.
avoidIf: Active malignancy concern, pregnancy, or clinician-advised avoidance of experimental peptides.
timingOrSequencingNotes: Render as a paired research note; do not encode exact co-dosing.

citationRefs.title: Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and Wound Healing
Thymosin beta-4: a multi-functional regenerative peptide. Basic properties and clinical applications

rationale: Cagrilintide plus semaglutide has direct randomized human evidence as CagriSema, combining amylin and GLP-1 mechanisms.
expectedSynergy: Distinct satiety and gastric-emptying pathways can produce greater weight loss than either component alone in trials.
safetyCaveats: Additive GI adverse events, dehydration risk, hypoglycemia risk when used with insulin/secretagogues, and pancreatitis/gallbladder warning context.
avoidIf: Personal/family MTC or MEN2 risk, severe gastroparesis, pancreatitis history unless clinician-reviewed, pregnancy, or concurrent GLP-1/GIP/glucagon agonist use.
timingOrSequencingNotes: Represent as coadministration evidence; do not encode self-mixing or dosing instructions.

Cagrilintide, a dual amylin agonist, for weight management

rationale: Semaglutide plus cagrilintide is the reciprocal CagriSema pairing with direct randomized human evidence.
expectedSynergy: Amylin-receptor agonism plus GLP-1 receptor agonism for stronger satiety and weight-management effects.
safetyCaveats: Additive GI adverse events, dehydration risk, hypoglycemia risk when used with insulin/secretagogues, and pancreatitis/gallbladder warning context.
avoidIf: Personal/family MTC or MEN2 risk, severe gastroparesis, pancreatitis history unless clinician-reviewed, pregnancy, or concurrent GLP-1/GIP/glucagon agonist use.
timingOrSequencingNotes: Represent as coadministration evidence; do not encode self-mixing or dosing instructions.

rationale: hCG can support intratesticular testosterone signaling during testosterone-induced gonadotropin suppression.
expectedSynergy: Exogenous testosterone symptom replacement plus LH-like testicular signaling support.
safetyCaveats: Can raise estradiol, acne, gynecomastia, mood changes, and polycythemia risk context. Requires clinician monitoring.
avoidIf: Active prostate/breast cancer, untreated severe sleep apnea, high hematocrit, uncontrolled heart failure, or no fertility/testicular-preservation goal.
timingOrSequencingNotes: Avoid protocol dosing; monitoring guidance belongs in the later adjunct/monitoring phase.

citationRefs.title: Testosterone replacement therapy: a review of benefits and risks
Co-administration of HCG with testosterone replacement therapy

rationale: hCG supplies LH-like signaling and hMG supplies FSH/LH activity; combined gonadotropin therapy is established for spermatogenesis induction in male hypogonadotropic hypogonadism.
expectedSynergy: Leydig-cell testosterone support plus Sertoli-cell/spermatogenesis support.
safetyCaveats: Requires semen, testosterone, estradiol, LH/FSH, and testicular-volume context; can raise estradiol and gynecomastia risk.
avoidIf: Primary testicular failure without specialist plan, active hormone-sensitive cancer, pregnancy exposure concerns, or unsupervised fertility treatment.
timingOrSequencingNotes: Attach semen analysis and hormone-lab monitoring in the later monitoring-support phase.

citationRefs.title: Co-administration of HCG with testosterone replacement therapy
HMG and HCG combination therapy for male hypogonadotropic hypogonadism

rationale: Ipamorelin is a selective GHS-R agonist and Sermorelin is a GHRH analog; this is the cleanest current in-catalog GH secretagogue pairing.
expectedSynergy: Selective GHS-R stimulation plus GHRH receptor stimulation for physiologic GH pulse support.
safetyCaveats: Monitor IGF-1/glucose; avoid redundant GH-axis exposure.
avoidIf: Elevated IGF-1, uncontrolled diabetes, active malignancy, untreated sleep apnea, pregnancy, or concurrent HGH.
timingOrSequencingNotes: Best current in-catalog GH pairing but still not direct outcome evidence.

citationRefs.title: Ipamorelin, the first selective growth hormone secretagogue
Sermorelin (GRF 1-29) in aging: growth hormone secretagogue overview

rationale: Pinealon is discussed as a neural / EDR bioregulator for oxidative-stress defense and cognitive resilience, while Epitalon is framed around pineal melatonin and telomere-related longevity signaling.
expectedSynergy: Complementary short-course bioregulator themes: neural resilience (Pinealon) plus circadian / pineal framing (Epitalon) without assuming a validated dual-drug effect.
safetyCaveats: No controlled human combination trial for Pinealon + Epitalon. Both are research peptides without US FDA cognitive/longevity labels; stack only as research metadata.
avoidIf: Pregnancy, known hypersensitivity to either peptide, or clinician-advised avoidance of experimental peptides.
timingOrSequencingNotes: Community charts often place both in short 10–20 day morning courses with long rests; do not encode exact co-dosing.

citationRefs.title: EDR Peptide: Possible Mechanism of Gene Expression and Protein Synthesis Regulation Involved in the Pathogenesis of Alzheimer's Disease
Epithalon peptide induces telomerase activity and telomere elongation in human somatic cells

rationale: Semax is commonly stacked for BDNF-linked focus and learning, while Pinealon is positioned as slower background neuroprotection and oxidative-stress support.
expectedSynergy: Acute cognitive signaling (Semax) plus ultrashort bioregulator neuroprotection themes (Pinealon); theoretical only.
safetyCaveats: No controlled combination RCTs. Semax can be activating for some users; avoid late dosing if sleep is disrupted. Neither has a US FDA cognitive label.
avoidIf: Pregnancy, seizure-disorder caution without clinician input, or hypersensitivity to either peptide.
timingOrSequencingNotes: Render as a paired research note; do not encode exact co-dosing or assume superior bioavailability of either route.
